title: Chapter Seventy-Three: Zhu Lianting
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

He felt gazes as soon as he entered. Not just one or two.

Energy waves seemed contained in every gaze. Tremendous pressure. Dozens were seated in the front courtyard serving as a banquet hall. The prestigious family's unique refined spirit could be felt. Even at a glance, there was no one who was not extraordinary.

Among them, one person particularly stood out.

"You bastard."

A middle-aged woman wearing jet-black palace clothes parted her red lips. The silk wrapping her body was dyed with black lacquer. Dignity overflowed from her beautiful face. She wrapped herself in noble energy waves as if having learned special techniques, and her gaze seemed to look down on all under heaven.

"Are you the child using the Jeong surname?"

She asked from the seat of honor. The deliberate mention of the Jeong surname. Her inner thoughts were slightly visible.

Yeon-shin did not answer. He turned his head.

"Is this your mother?"

"...That's right," Ma Se-in said, glum.

*Zhu Lianting.* Imperial blood, however distant. Married into the Ma family's branch line, but carrying herself as though she had conquered it.

"Are you from the imperial family?" Yeon-shin asked her directly.

"That is correct."

He understood the circumstances instantly. Jade ornaments pinned her hair up. Round Han ears -- distant branch of imperial bloodline. Emperor Taizu had over forty consorts, and the numerous descendants without connection to imperial power were free to take wives and concubines. Zhu Lianting must be the daughter of one such person -- noble and insignificant at once.

"I greet Lady Zhu."

He paid respects but did not prostrate himself. She was an outsider by marriage. Even a trace of Taizu's blood did not change that. She had left Beijing. She was no longer a person of the Forbidden City. She was a person of the Desolate Ma family.

A martial artist.

"What an impudent fellow."

The corners of Zhu Lianting's mouth curled up. The reaction was unexpected. Since she had used "you bastard," he had thought she was someone desperate to protect her son's successor rights. The surrounding family members also did not act rashly. They silently kept their eyes on Yeon-shin.

"Sit comfortably. This gathering is for you."

"...Then, thank you."

He briefly bowed and sat at the banquet seat. Ma Se-in quietly followed and sat beside his mother. Musicians entered and began playing classical melodies from far away, faintly filling the gathering's rank.

"I wanted to test your capacity," Zhu Lianting said.

He waited.

"It's been twenty years since I left Beijing but I'm like this. The authority still ingrained in my conduct sometimes hurts people's hearts, so I hope you will be generous in understanding."

Her words were unconventional. An imperial woman acknowledging her own sharpness. What was her intention?

While plucked zither strings and echoing flutes formed gentle melodies, those seated at the banquet were briefly silent. Just the sound, though -- the world of masters was different. Yeon-shin felt streams of internal energy endlessly scanning him. Secretive enough that he could barely detect them due to having energy sense above martial arts level. He deliberately did not show his awareness.

"I'm curious about your business."

He asked directly. Both sides hid their intentions. But it was a gathering where the inviting side was in need. There was no reason to bow and enter.

A smile formed on Zhu Lianting's lips.

"I am accustomed to speaking indirectly from birth, so a warrior's way of speaking feels unfamiliar. Many have praised you. Saying you're material to be a pillar of Desolate Sect." She reached for a white jade bottle. "When you had your coming-of-age ceremony. What your intention was in refusing the main family's successor position."

Yeon-shin gained much information from her few words. Two purposes at this gathering.

First: martial arts. They wanted to know where his power came from. He had reached blue rank at the youngest age in the shortest time. His original martial arts -- none of them Desolate Ma family martial arts. It was so even before receiving cultivation techniques from the Sect Lord. The stories of recent missions and the Sect Lord's dramatic return would add to their questions.

*Why did she carry me all the way to the main sect...* Because gratitude was greater, it was hard to complain even inwardly.

Second. The main point.

"You asked about my intention." Yeon-shin calmly opened his mouth. "I believe I already declared it that day. Since my established will lies with the murim and main sect, I don't want to be bound to my maternal prestigious family. Ma Se-in will make an excellent family head."

"Your nature is quite magnanimous," Zhu Lianting said, covering her mouth as she laughed. An elegant bearing.

He stared at her. She was one who had called the Desolate Ma family to the main sect. A distant branch imperial family member completely settling in the Ma family and acting as mistress.

Rather, this was good. Like this, there was no need to make enemies deliberately. Drawing a line would be enough.

"So, I hope not to be entangled with your family going forward. What I want is to sever ties. That would help avoid useless discord."

Light silence. The smile disappeared from Zhu Lianting's face. Ma Se-in's expression stiffened. Only the instruments' melodies flowed through the banquet hall.

*Shouldn't they be the ones making this request?*

"Isn't this the family that already severed ties with my mother? Please answer. Before I publicly open my mouth to slash the Ma family's honor."

He spoke deliberately excessive words. Yet only questioning lingered on Zhu Lianting's face.

"How can that be? Do you think the martial arts you received from the Sect Lord guarantees your authority? Wrong. Desolate Sect is not such a place."

"I just want to live fully as a Radiant Demon Wing warrior. I don't want the Desolate Ma family name attached before my alias. Isn't this welcome news for Lady Zhu?"

"You speak of difficult matters so calmly." She spoke slowly. "Why is that?"

"Because they are simple."

"You have already stepped into the main family's political strife. It was bound to happen the moment you showed talent while having Ma family blood. Those who say we must take you as successor, those who argue you should be made into a sword, we who don't want you."

A faint smile.

"Either way. The moment you sever ties, great winds blow over responsibility. If you don't want to face headwinds, you'll have no choice but to do anything."

"This is ridiculous."

"Aren't you the Sect Lord's direct disciple? *Child.* Your existence is already that significant. Even if you alone speak of severing ties, the main family will cling to your robes."

"It's difficult to remain seated," Yeon-shin said.

He stood. Turned his back. Though he rudely turned his back, no one thundered at him. In the realm of politics, they were clearly masters when discussing martial might.

The musicians' unchanging performance saw him off.

The evening air hit his face and the crown-point headache flared behind his eyes -- the upper dantian's reminder that his body was crumbling from within even as the world outside demanded more and more of it.

*She is right. That is the worst part. She is right, and I cannot afford the time this will cost me.*

---
